spec_match.rationale: The BroadcastChannel check incorrectly expects an exception to be thrown during construction for opaque origins. Since the constructor succeeds and partitions instead, the page reports a false-negative same-origin isolation leak even in fully compliant Chrome 150+ environments.

interactive.rationale: Includes a direct test execution button, live browser/UA checks, status logs, and a detailed side-by-side comparison results grid.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the parent description of comparing worker origins before/after Chrome 150.

edge_cases.rationale: All local navigation and breadcrumb links use the wrong, long directory slug, leading to 404 failures when clicked.

references.rationale: Cites the correct ChromeStatus link under see also.

design_system.rationale: Design is great, employing clean stonewall panels, neat status grid comparisons, and standard badge elements.

openQuestions.title: Fix BroadcastChannel false-leak reporting logic
openQuestions.detail: The BroadcastChannel constructor does not raise an exception in opaque contexts; it instantiates successfully in a partitioned scope. Correct the verification logic to send a message from the worker and prove that the main thread fails to receive it after a short timeout, rather than treating a lack of exceptions as a same-origin security failure.

openQuestions.title: Fix broken folder slugs in crumbs and links
openQuestions.detail: All crumb and navigation links contain the long slug '/v150/opaque-origin-for-data-url-dedicated-and-shared-workers/' (resulting in 404). Replace them with '/v150/opaque-origin-for-data-urls/' to restore correct site navigation.
